title: अयोध्या स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अयोध्या स्तोत्रम् ॥

अयोध्यां प्रणमामि तां रामजन्मभूमिं शुभाम्।
सरयूतटसंशोभां पुण्यधामं सनातनीम्॥ १॥

सप्तपुरीषु मुख्यां तां मोक्षदायिनीं पुरीम्।
रामचन्द्रपदाङ्कितां अयोध्यां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ २॥

दशरथनृपवासं कौसल्यानन्दसद्म।
सुमित्राकैकयीभूषां अयोध्यां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ३॥

यत्र रामो जगन्नाथो बालरूपेण लीलया।
क्रीडते भ्रातृभिः सार्धं अयोध्यां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ४॥

गुरुवशिष्ठसंसेव्यां यज्ञभूमिं तपोधनाम्।
वेदघोषसमन्वितां अयोध्यां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ५॥

रामराज्यप्रतिष्ठायां धर्मस्य परमं पदम्।
न्यायस्य निलयं शुद्धां अयोध्यां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ६॥

सरयूजलपूतां तां हनूमद्भक्तिशोभिताम्।
सीतारामविहारस्थलं अयोध्यां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ७॥

कोटिसूर्यसमप्रभं रामजन्मभूमिमन्दिरम्।
त्रिलोकपूजितं दिव्यं अयोध्यां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ८॥

अयोध्यास्तोत्रमिदं शुभं यः पठेद्भक्तिपूर्वकम्।
रामकृपां समाप्नोति मोक्षद्वारं लभेत् ध्रुवम्॥ ९॥

॥ इति श्री अयोध्या स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ayodhya Stotram ||

Ayodhyam pranamami tam Rama-janma-bhumim shubham |
Sarayu-tata-samshobham punya-dhamam sanatanim || 1 ||

Sapta-purishu mukhyam tam moksha-dayinim purim |
Ramachandra-padankitam Ayodhyam prananamyaham || 2 ||

Dasharatha-nripa-vasam Kausalya-ananda-sadma |
Sumitra-Kaikeyi-bhusham Ayodhyam prananamyaham || 3 ||

Yatra Ramo Jagannathao bala-rupena lilaya |
Kridate bhratribhih sardham Ayodhyam prananamyaham || 4 ||

Guru-Vashishtha-samsevyam yajna-bhumim tapo-dhanam |
Veda-ghosha-samanvitam Ayodhyam prananamyaham || 5 ||

Rama-rajya-pratishthayam dharmasya paramam padam |
Nyayasya nilayam shudham Ayodhyam prananamyaham || 6 ||

Sarayu-jala-putam tam Hanumad-bhakti-shobhitam |
Sita-Rama-vihara-sthalam Ayodhyam prananamyaham || 7 ||

Koti-surya-samaprabham Rama-janma-bhumi-mandiram |
Triloka-pujitam divyam Ayodhyam prananamyaham || 8 ||

Ayodhya-stotram-idam shubham yah pathed-bhakti-purvakam |
Rama-kripam samapnoti moksha-dvaram labhet dhruvam || 9 ||

|| Iti Shri Ayodhya Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
राम की जन्मभूमि, शुभ, सरयू तट से शोभित, पुण्य धाम, सनातनी अयोध्या को प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ १॥

सात पुरियों में प्रमुख, मोक्षदायिनी पुरी, रामचन्द्र के चरणों से अंकित अयोध्या को प्रणाम॥ २॥

राजा दशरथ का निवास, कौसल्या के आनन्द का सदन, सुमित्रा और कैकयी से भूषित अयोध्या को प्रणाम॥ ३॥

जहाँ जगन्नाथ राम बाल रूप में लीला करते हुए भाइयों के साथ क्रीड़ा करते हैं, उस अयोध्या को प्रणाम॥ ४॥

गुरु वशिष्ठ द्वारा सेवित, यज्ञभूमि, तपोधन, वेद-ध्वनि से गुंजित अयोध्या को प्रणाम॥ ५॥

रामराज्य की स्थापना में धर्म का परम पद, न्याय का निलय, शुद्ध अयोध्या को प्रणाम॥ ६॥

सरयू जल से पवित्र, हनुमान की भक्ति से शोभित, सीता-राम के विहार स्थल अयोध्या को प्रणाम॥ ७॥

कोटि सूर्य के समान प्रभावान रामजन्मभूमि मन्दिर, त्रिलोक पूजित, दिव्य अयोध्या को प्रणाम॥ ८॥

जो भक्तिपूर्वक इस शुभ अयोध्या स्तोत्र का पाठ करता है, वह राम की कृपा प्राप्त करता है और मोक्ष का द्वार अवश्य पाता है॥ ९॥
benefits: Prayer to the sacred city of Ayodhya, birthplace of Lord Rama
Invokes the blessing of the merit of ayodhya pilgrimage
Invokes blessings of Lord Rama and opens the door to moksha
Traditionally associated with peace, dharma, and righteousness into life
Ideal for recitation during Ram Navami and Ayodhya pilgrimage
Connects the devotee with the spiritual energy of the holy Dham
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Rama, Ayodhya Stotram (अयोध्या स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. Attributed to Traditional Rama Literature, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to bestow the merit of Ayodhya pilgrimage, bring peace, dharma, and righteousness into life, and connect the devotee with the spiritual energy of the holy Dham. This sacred text is ideally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ram Navami and Vivah Panchami for enhanced blessings. Comprising 9 verses, it can be completed in approximately 8 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Ayodhya Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Ayodhya Stotram - prayer to the sacred city of Ayodhya Dham in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Birthplace of Lord Rama.
relatedSearches: Ayodhya Stotram Sanskrit lyrics with meaning
Ayodhya Mahatmya Skanda Purana Vaishnava Khanda
Ayodhya Sapta Puri seven moksha cities
Sarayu river snan punya pitru tarpana
Ayodhya Stotram for Rama Navami recitation

seoFaqs.question: What is the canonical scriptural source of the Ayodhya Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The principal source is the Ayodhya Mahatmya, the tenth section of the Vaishnava Khanda of the Skanda Purana, comprising approximately 990 shlokas across ten chapters. This text frames Ayodhya as one of the seven moksha-puris (Sapta Puri: Ayodhya, Mathura, Maya/Haridwar, Kashi, Kanchi, Avantika/Ujjain, Dvarka), enumerates its sacred tirthas including Sahasradhara, Brahma Kund, Vidya Kund and the Ram Janmabhumi itself, and embeds several stutis to the city personified as a goddess and to its principal deity Rama. The popular Ayodhya Stotram circulating today extracts and reorganises these embedded stutis into a recitable composition. The Padma Purana and Brahma Purana also contain shorter Ayodhya-mahatmya passages drawn upon in supplementary verses. (Sources: wisdomlib.org Skanda Purana Ayodhya; kamakoti.org; thecommunemag.com; Wikipedia Ayodhya.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Ayodhya counted among the Sapta Puri liberation cities?
seoFaqs.answer: The Skanda Purana states that residence in or even death at Ayodhya guarantees moksha, placing it on a par with Kashi for liberation. Three reasons are given in the Mahatmya: it is the avatara-bhumi of Lord Rama, the seventh and most-followed avatara of Vishnu, whose birth here sanctifies the soil; it is the capital of the Ikshvaku Suryavanshi dynasty which produced sixty generations of dharma-rajas before Rama including Manu, Ikshvaku, Sagara, Bhagiratha, Raghu, Aja and Dasharatha; and the Sarayu river that flows past Ayodhya is held to issue from Brahma Kund and is more sacred than the Ganga at Gaya for shraddha-tarpana for ancestors, conferring sadyo-mukti on those whose final rites are performed on its banks. (Sources: kamakoti.org; tirthayatra.org Sarayu; Britannica Ayodhya; Wikipedia Ayodhya.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the name Ayodhya as etymologically explained in the Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sanskrit name Ayodhya is parsed as a + yodhya, the un-warrable or invincible city, derived from the root yudh (to fight). The Skanda Purana explains that the city was founded by Manu Vaivasvata using a divinely-given plan and was protected by such pervasive dharma that no enemy army could ever conquer it, a statement borne out in the Ramayana where the city is besieged conceptually by Bharata's grief but never militarily. The alternative ancient name Saket, attested in early Buddhist Pali texts, derives from sa-keta meaning that-which-bears-a-banner, referring to its status as the capital city. The Stotram uses both names interchangeably to invoke both the dharmic invincibility and the imperial dignity of Rama's birthplace. (Sources: Britannica Ayodhya; Wikipedia Ayodhya; grokipedia.com Ayodhya; thecommunemag.com.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the principal tirthas of Ayodhya enumerated in the Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mahatmya enumerates dozens of tirthas; the most-cited in the Stotram are the Ram Janmabhumi (the actual birth-spot of Rama), Sahasradhara Tirtha (the thousand-streams tirtha said to confer the merit of all tirthas at once), Brahma Kund (where Brahma performed yajna), Sita Kund and Sita Rasoi (associated with Sita), Hanuman Garhi (the fortress shrine of Hanuman), Kanak Bhavan (the golden palace gifted by Kaikeyi to Sita), Treta Ke Thakur (image-set marking the Ashvamedha site), Nageshwarnath (the Shiva-linga set up by Kusha, son of Rama), and Guptar Ghat (the spot of Rama's final water-entry into the Sarayu). Pilgrims completing the 14-kosi parikrama recite the Stotram while visiting these sites in sequence. (Sources: tirthayatra.org Sahasradhara; tirthayatra.org Sarayu; srjbtkshetra.org; travelwisesr.com.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the most auspicious time to recite Ayodhya Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Rama Navami (Chaitra Shukla Navami, March-April), the appearance day of Rama, is the supreme occasion. The Stotram is recited multiple times that day and forms part of the temple liturgy at Ram Janmabhumi. Other powerful days include the Chaitra and Kartik Purnimas, on which the major 14-kosi parikrama is performed; Sita Navami in Vaishakha Shukla Navami; the Karthik month in entirety, when the Sarayu deepotsava is observed; and any Ekadashi during chaturmasa. The Stotram is also a daily recitation for Vaishnava households, particularly those of the Ramanandi sampradaya based in Ayodhya, who chant it as the morning pratah-smarana before opening the temple-doors of household Rama-shrines. (Sources: kamakoti.org; tirthayatra.org Sarayu; srjbtkshetra.org.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Ayodhya Stotram differ from the Ramayana-derived Rama-stutis?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ayodhya Stotram is kshetra-mahatmya: it praises the city itself as a sacred geography rather than focusing primarily on Rama's biography. While Rama-stutis like the Rama Raksha Stotram, Rama Hridayam, or Tulsidas's Ramcharitmanas Aratis praise Rama's qualities (his beauty, dharma, victory over Ravana, etc.), the Ayodhya Stotram praises the soil, rivers, ghats, kunds, mandirs and the personified goddess of the city; Rama appears as the indweller and consort of Ayodhya rather than as the sole subject. Devotees often pair the two: Rama Raksha Stotram for personal protection through the deity, Ayodhya Stotram for sanctification of one's pilgrimage and purification through identification with the holy city. (Sources: kamakoti.org; wisdomlib.org Skanda Purana; srjbtkshetra.org.)
